dublinCore.dc:title: 117 HR 9388 : One-Stop Pilot Program Extension Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Received; read twice and referred to the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation

form.official-title: To amend title 49, United States Code, to authorize an extension of a program to permit screened passengers and their property arriving on direct flights or flight segments originating at certain foreign last point of departure airports to continue on additional flights or flight segments originating in the United States without additional screening, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the One-Stop Pilot Program Extension Act.

subsection.text: Subsection (d) of section 7132 of the James M. Inhofe National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2023 (Public Law 117–263; 49 U.S.C. 44901 note) is amended to read as follows:

Subject to subsection (f), the Administrator, in coordination with the Commissioner of U.S. Customs and Border Protection (CBP), may permit checked baggage arriving on direct flights or flight segments arriving from participating foreign last point of departure airports referred to in subsection (b) to continue on additional flights or flight segments originating in the United States without additional TSA security re-screening if the following requirements are satisfied:

paragraph.text: The initial checked baggage screening at such an airport was conducted using an explosives detection system in accordance with an aviation security screening agreement described in subsection (e).
Passengers arriving from such airports are unable to access their checked baggage until arrival at their final destination.

CBP has received timely images of such baggage prior to the arrival in the United States of such passengers and baggage that satisfy CBP requirements from such an airport corresponding to the checked baggage of such passengers to ensure appropriate border security enforcement can be completed in an efficient manner.
CBP has not identified any such passenger or baggage for further inspection.

subsection.text: Subsection (k) of section 7132 of the James M. Inhofe National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2023 is amended by striking six and inserting ten.

attestation-group.attestation-date: Passed the House of Representatives July 13, 2026.
